## Accepted

## Final Accepted Amendments

### `fr-11-2824764e`

finding_summary: Failed `iterative_minimal` iterations are journaled under the wrong invocation id. `execute_stage_with_retry()` always emits `stage_failed` with `history_record_base_id(...)` (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:5782`), but iterative implementer calls are actually invoked with suffixed ids from `invocation_id_for_stage(..., Some("it{iteration}"))` (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:7103`). When an implementer iteration fails, `stage_failed.details.invocation_id` and the matching runtime log entry no longer identify the real raw output artifact for that failure, which breaks failure forensics and any tooling/operators that follow the recorded invocation id to inspect the backend output. remediation_hint: thread the actual invocation id (or the iteration suffix) back out of the iterative invocation path so the failure event records the same id the backend used.
